description: Violent Cretaceous thunderstorm at night — heavy volumetric fog, electric blue lightning, dark storm sky. Maximum tension and danger atmosphere for dinosaur encounters.
emotional_intent: Primal fear. The player feels exposed and hunted. Visibility is reduced. Every shadow could hide a predator.
cinematography_reference: Roger Deakins — No Country for Old Men night scenes. RDR2 thunderstorm lighting.

exponential_height_fog.description: Heavy storm fog — reduces visibility to ~150m, creates oppressive atmosphere

spawned_lights.label: Light_Storm_Lightning_001
spawned_lights.purpose: Simulates ambient lightning glow between strikes — keeps scene readable without full darkness

spawned_lights.label: Light_Storm_RainFill_001
spawned_lights.purpose: Simulates diffuse light through rain — creates wet surface sheen and volumetric rain shafts

lumen_settings.notes: Lumen reflections critical for wet ground puddle reflections during storm

recommended_primary.name: Thunder and rain during the night in the Amazonian Rainforest
recommended_primary.notes: 1-hour loop — perfect for sustained storm ambience. Amazonian rainforest = closest real-world analog to Cretaceous jungle.

recommended_accent.name: Thunder.wav
recommended_accent.notes: Short dramatic thunder crack — use as MetaSound trigger for lightning flash sync

gameplay_implications.dinosaur_behavior_modifier: predators more active in storm (reduced player visibility = hunting advantage)

palette_sequence.cycle_008: DuskPalette — orange-amber sun, warm volumetric fog
palette_sequence.cycle_009: DawnPalette — rose-gold sun, light shafts through ruins
palette_sequence.cycle_010: MiddayPalette — white-hot sun, heat haze, harsh shadows
palette_sequence.cycle_011: StormyNightPalette — cold blue-grey, heavy fog, lightning (THIS CYCLE)
palette_sequence.cycle_012_target: ClearNightPalette — moonlight silver, star glow, bioluminescent plants
